name: Zabaykalsky Krai (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the courage of the inhabitants of Transbaikal and the Cossack heritage of the region
the gold of the sun and the krai's mineral wealth

description: A field divided per pall (three regions meeting at the centre of the flag): a green triangle above, a red triangle below, and a yellow isoceles triangle along the hoist whose apex reaches the centre.
history: Adopted on 19 December 2008 by the Legislative Assembly of Zabaykalsky Krai shortly after the krai was formed (1 March 2008) by the merger of Chita Oblast and the Agin-Buryat Autonomous Okrug.

features.symbolism: the field is divided per pall into three triangles — green above for the taiga forests of Transbaikal, red below for the courage of the inhabitants and the Cossack heritage, and a yellow hoist triangle for the rising sun and the gold of the historic Transbaikal Cossack Host
